# Data generating function for univariate plsR models

This function generates a single univariate response value \\Y\\ and a
vector of explanatory variables \\(X_1,\ldots,X\_{totdim})\\ drawn from
a model with a given number of latent components.

## Usage

``` r
simul_data_UniYX(totdim, ncomp)
```

## Arguments

- totdim:

  Number of columns of the X vector (from `ncomp` to hardware limits)

- ncomp:

  Number of latent components in the model (from 2 to 6)

## Value

- vector:

  \\(Y,X_1,\ldots,X\_{totdim})\\

## Details

This function should be combined with the replicate function to give
rise to a larger dataset. The algorithm used is a port of the one
described in the article of Li which is a multivariate generalization of
the algorithm of Naes and Martens.

## References

T. Naes, H. Martens, Comparison of prediction methods for multicollinear
data, Commun. Stat., Simul. 14 (1985) 545-576.  
Morris, Elaine B. Martin, Model selection for partial least squares
regression, Chemometrics and Intelligent Laboratory Systems 64 (2002)
79-89,
[doi:10.1016/S0169-7439(02)00051-5](https://doi.org/10.1016/S0169-7439%2802%2900051-5)
.

## See also

[`simul_data_YX`](https://fbertran.github.io/plsRglm/reference/simul_data_YX.md)
and
[`simul_data_complete`](https://fbertran.github.io/plsRglm/reference/simul_data_complete.md)
for generating multivariate data

## Author

Frédéric Bertrand  
<frederic.bertrand@lecnam.net>  
<https://fbertran.github.io/homepage/>

## Examples

``` r
simul_data_UniYX(20,6)                          
#>           Y          X1          X2          X3          X4          X5 
#>  5.37208159  2.61772471  1.98015086  2.73966905 -0.65033218  2.80462160 
#>          X6          X7          X8          X9         X10         X11 
#>  2.56459761  2.97305937 -0.07073625  0.09994084  1.52460131  1.87522259 
#>         X12         X13         X14         X15         X16         X17 
#>  1.47017902  2.54432359  3.69661409  0.10830547  1.51483474  1.89846468 
#>         X18         X19         X20 
#>  1.46503159  2.55933333  3.67439144 

# \donttest{
dimX <- 6
Astar <- 2
simul_data_UniYX(dimX,Astar)
#>          Y         X1         X2         X3         X4         X5         X6 
#> -13.907393   1.960788   1.972129 -12.548192   1.981423   1.972791 -12.529984 
(dataAstar2 <- data.frame(t(replicate(50,simul_data_UniYX(dimX,Astar)))))
#>              Y           X1          X2          X3           X4           X5

#> 2    2.1036123  -4.23745332  -4.2451625   2.6827753  -4.23773593  -4.24655183

#> Error in eval(mf, parent.frame()): object 'dataAstar2' not found

dimX <- 6
Astar <- 3
simul_data_UniYX(dimX,Astar)
#>        Y       X1       X2       X3       X4       X5       X6 
#> 5.147520 1.513335 4.440176 2.109769 1.526822 4.455568 2.118202

dimX <- 6
Astar <- 4
simul_data_UniYX(dimX,Astar)
```
